title: NOC 21232 – Software Developers and Programmers | GTR Immigration
description: Software Developers and Programmers are among the most in-demand professionals across Canada, especially in major technology hubs such as Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Classified under NOC 21232 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for multiple immigration pathways including Express Entry–linked programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) options.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2123 – Computer, software, and web designers and developers

duties: Design, write, test, and debug software code
Analyze system requirements and propose upgrades
Develop mobile, web, and e-commerce applications
Maintain gameplay features for digital platforms
Prepare documentation for new and updated software
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, software engineering, or a related field
Or a college program with strong programming components
Career progression into software-engineering roles with experience
job_outlook: This keeps the occupation high-priority for immigration, especially for overseas professionals with:
additional_information: Canada’s rapidly expanding digital economy, startup ecosystem, and enterprise-software sector continue to fuel strong recruitment for:
Full-stack developersMobile-app programmersGame developersMultimedia engineersE-commerce specialistsInteractive-media developers
Annual salaries typically range from $45,406–$113,458 CAD, depending on province, experience level, and technical specialization.
